summary: Excellent handheld‑DAT capture of a vibrant 1991 show, with the band delivering tight, energetic ensemble playing.
review: Both reviewers award five stars, praising the Marcus Buick hand‑held DAT source as exceptionally clear and lively. The performance is described as an under‑appreciated gem from the Dead's later halcyon period, with every member—from Jerry’s “scuba diving” guitar to Vince and Bruce’s synchronized keyboards—playing with renewed vigor.

ai_rating.rationale: Consistently high (5‑star) reviews highlight superb recording fidelity and a strong, cohesive performance; weighting favors the outstanding show with excellent capture.

recording_quality.technical_notes: Hand‑held DAT recording made by Marcus Buick from front of board; reviewers note crystal‑clear sound and high fidelity despite being an audience‑sourced tape.

show_quality.setlist_flow: Reviewers emphasize overall ensemble cohesion and vigor, noting the show as a tightly knit, under‑estimated performance from this era.

band_member_comments.Jerry: Described as “scuba diving Jerry,” indicating adventurous, energetic playing.
band_member_comments.Phil: Praised for renewed vigor (referred to as Bobby/Philly), showing strong bass work.
band_member_comments.Bob: Noted for newly found vigor alongside the rest of the band.
band_member_comments.Keys: Vince and Bruce are highlighted as being in‑sync and driving the performance forward.
band_member_comments.Drums: Both Mickey (and Bill) receive commendation for their vigorous, tight drumming.
